title: Causal Observability, Freshness SLOs, Capacity, and Cost · Goroutine Forensics
summary: A preserved Go feed-service run contains one plausible discovery, URL, XML, normalization, fetch, SQL, transaction, lease, goroutine, handler, accessibility, migration, or release defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in privacy-minimized feed-service operations report and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging,…
objectives: Correlate request, tenant-safe source key, schedule claim, fetch attempt, validator, parse, transaction, and response without leaking raw private identifiers.
Emit bounded structured events with causal error identity and tested redaction for URLs, content, queries, headers, tokens, SQL, and personal state.
Define availability, successful-poll, publisher-respect, ingestion-lag, reader-latency, and restore indicators with windows and error budgets.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Feed Go Capacity Model
steps.instruction:
Order the Feed Go Capacity Model Feed aggregation and Go source-to-reader-and-recovery evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Feed Go Capacity Model.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Feed Go Capacity Model assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Feed aggregation and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Feed Go Capacity Model, keep Feed Go Capacity Model connected to executable Go feed, XML, HTTP, PostgreSQL, schedule, API, reader, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.text: A debug Go team handles case 2602 during an accessible community-news reader review. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove privacy-minimized causal telemetry, freshness SLOs, bounded cardinality, and representative capacity; failed, canceled, stale, and cross-tenant operations must preserve the prior admitted state; then change one schema, constraint, transaction, upsert, migration, query, or search plan and preserve admitted state. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Load representative publishers, item sizes, update rates, tenants, reads, searches, failures, and restores against CPU, memory, connections, locks, and budgets.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Feed Go Capacity Model has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

options.text: Another successful run of the same happy-path example. That claim does not survive the feed go capacity model · fault isolation evidence boundary.

For feed go capacity model · fault isolation, import one convenient trusted RSS feed, omit reader and publisher outcomes, revision, discovery and SSRF scope, Atom and namespace cases, XML and HTTP budgets, PostgreSQL constraints and transaction, schedule, lease, goroutine cleanup, tenant, accessibility, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as feed-service proof.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Feed Go Capacity Model
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: feed-go-capacity-model-kb2ex9" that uses case 4758 to prove privacy-minimized causal telemetry, freshness SLOs, bounded cardinality, and representative capacity. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, parse through native XML libraries, contact publishers, access PostgreSQL, read host files, handle real signals, launch unbounded goroutines, or claim race, load, accessibility, container, backup, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5, pgx 5.10.0, goose 3.27.2, PostgreSQL 18.4, original fixtures, controlled RoundTrippers and httptest servers, disposable databases, race and fuzz runs, subprocesses, inspected images, accessibility review, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, and release gates.

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Feed Go Capacity Model.
